scope: Phase G: full generated decode block tile through AMDISARenderer
route_attribution: AMDISARenderer selected for the tile; no HIP/LLVM/owned fallback in candidate route
predicate_strategy: bool = 0/1 in a VGPR; CMP -> v_cmp(VCC) + v_cndmask(0,1); WHERE -> v_cmp_ne(cond,0) + v_cndmask(f,t)
gated_store_strategy: EXEC predication: v_cmp_ne(gate)->VCC; s_and_saveexec_b32(s5, VCC); store; s_mov(EXEC,s5)
cdiv_cmod_strategy: constant power-of-two only (tile uses /2,%2): CDIV->v_lshrrev(k), CMOD->v_and(n-1); non-pow2 fails loudly
fault_site: output store loop (and every other counted loop): branch targets resolved to the wrong loop
offset_provenance: addressing probe (store d2=lane*R+dd2 in place of the accumulator) showed only EVEN d2 written -> the output dd2 loop (range R=2) executed once. Disasm: loop counters s41/s42 were SHARED by multiple loops, and lower_range/lower_end keyed top/out labels by the physical counter index -> the label dict (last-wins) made one loop's backedge/exit branch into another loop's body. ctx.vreg gives each RANGE a unique VIRTUAL counter but regalloc reuses the PHYSICAL SGPR across non-overlapping loops, so the index is not a unique loop id.

bisection_table.real_score_path: non-deterministic + intermittent MMU fault (the label collision corrupts the score loops too)
bisection_table.const_score(0.1)+const_V(1.0): deterministic structure but output wrong -> isolated the bug downstream of score
bisection_table.addressing_probe(store d2): even positions written, odd NOT -> output loop runs 1/2 iterations = loop bug
bisection_table.Hkv=1 single GLOBAL-range iter: separate edge case: barrier elided + hang (bound-1 loop); NOT the main bug
bisection_table.Hkv>=2 (real): now PASS after the loop-label fix

minimal_repro: two sequential counted loops sharing a regalloc'd SGPR counter -> colliding top/out labels
fix_summary: renderer/isa/amd.py: key counted-loop top/out labels by a UNIQUE monotonic loop-label id (assigned per-RANGE at isel via _next_loop_label, carried in RANGE arg[2] and the lowered rep's tag[1]), NOT by the physical counter SGPR index (which regalloc reuses across non-overlapping loops). lower_range/lower_end now emit ('top'|'out', label_id). This also fixes the intermittent MMU fault (corrupt branch targets jumped into wrong loop bodies, producing out-of-bounds global addresses). Two earlier fixes still in place: staged-reduce group-segment n_threads (a48dec4ae) and half-LDS ds_store_b16 width.
staged_reduce_32_status: PASS (fixed: sole-lidx0 keeps SPECIAL reachable -> elf/renderer agree on n_threads; correct 4/8/16/32 + multi-warp)
half_lds_store_width_status: PASS (fixed: element size encoded as const src at isel; K/V staging now ds_store_b16, was b32 corrupting adjacent half slot)
loop_label_collision_status: PASS (fixed: counted-loop labels keyed by unique loop id, not the reused counter SGPR index)
first_divergent_stage_before_fix: output store loop wrote only even positions (dd2 loop ran 1/2 iterations)
remaining_deferred_work: Phase H-O (model route attribution, W==D, scheduling, promotion) -- now unblocked by G.
next_minimal_action: Phase G PASS. Next: Phase H (route-bound model W==D / decode integration) per the broader plan.
